name: Compliance: Traffic from DMZ to Internal Network
notes: This rule triggers when traffic is passed from the DMZ to an internal network.  This is typically not allowed under compliance regulations.  You should make sure the DMZ object in the network hierarchy in defined before enabling this rule.
conditions_text: when the context is Local to Local
when the source network is DMZ
when the destination network is DMZ
when a flow or an event matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Firewall or ACL Accept, BB:CategoryDefinition: Successful Communication, BB:DeviceDefinition: FW / Router / Switch
TD_list: APPLY Compliance: Traffic from DMZ to Internal Network on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the context is Local to Local
AND when the source network is DMZ
AND NOT when the destination network is DMZ
AND when a flow or an event matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Firewall or ACL Accept, BB:CategoryDefinition: Successful Communication, BB:DeviceDefinition: FW / Router / Switch
RED_list: Event name: Compliance: Traffic from DMZ to Internal Network
Event description: Detects when traffic is passed from the DMZ to an internal network.  This is typically not allowed under compliance regulations as it can expose the internet to unnecessary risk.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
